title: Arbiter of the Grade
description: Room 204 of Maple Heights Elementary. The Arbiter gives a pop quiz with personalized questions and rubric-based consequences.

lines: Room 204 was exactly as it should have been and exactly as it should never have been.
Desks in perfect rows. A chalkboard with nothing on it yet. A projector screen showing a single word: ASSESSMENT.
At the front of the room stood a figure in a gray blazer with a lanyard, holding a clipboard and looking at the party the way a teacher looks at students who arrive on the wrong day.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of the Grade
lines.text: You entered the room. You're enrolled.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of the Grade
lines.text: The questions are personalized. I have your files. I've had your files since before you arrived. The Warden sends reports.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: What are we being graded on?

lines.speaker: Arbiter of the Grade
lines.text: Competency under pressure. Adaptive reasoning. Resource allocation in suboptimal conditions.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of the Grade
lines.text: Also: how quickly you abandon your principles when threatened. That one's a trick question. The rubric rewards partial credit.

lines.speaker: Arbiter of the Grade
lines.text: You already know what failing looks like. Every party that has sat in these desks has known. Pencils out.
